channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Oyo confirms four dead, others injured in Ibadan clash, vows justice
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Oyo State confirms four dead and several injured in a violent Ibadan clash. The government vows to arrest and prosecute those responsible for the Ojoo area
Read More: https://punchng.com/oyo-confirms-four-dead-others-injured-in-ibadan-clash-vows-justice/

item.title: AU appoints humanitarian minister Doro to AfHA governing board
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigeria’s Humanitarian Minister, Bernard Doro, appointed to the African Humanitarian Agency (AfHA) Governing Board, representing West Africa for a three-ye
Read More: https://punchng.com/au-appoints-humanitarian-minister-doro-to-afha-governing-board/

item.title: FG rolls out rehabilitation services at PHCs nationwide
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Federal Government has launched integrated rehabilitation services at Primary Health Care centres nationwide, bringing essential care closer to million
Read More: https://punchng.com/fg-rolls-out-rehabilitation-services-at-phcs-nationwide/

item.title: Senate backs bill proposing 15-year jail term for fake drug dealers
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Nigerian Senate has passed a bill proposing a 15-year jail term for fake drug dealers and hawkers, aiming to curb counterfeit medicines and protect pub
Read More: https://punchng.com/senate-advances-bill-proposing-15-year-jail-term-for-fake-drug-dealers/

item.title: PFIPC scandal: Kano senator insists president’s ICPC probe not enough, demands Senate inquest
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Kano Senator Kawu Sumaila insists on a Senate probe into the N1.3bn PFIPC scandal, despite President Tinubu’s ICPC investigation, citing concerns over budg
Read More: https://punchng.com/pfipc-scandal-kano-senator-insists-presidents-icpc-probe-not-enough-demands-senate-inquest/

item.title: N1.3bn fraud: Ex-Port Harcourt refinery MD gets N150m bail
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Former Port Harcourt Refinery MD, Ahmed Dikko, has been granted N150m bail in an alleged N1.3bn fraud case involving money laundering and refinery rehabili
Read More: https://punchng.com/n1-3bn-fraud-ex-port-harcourt-refinery-md-gets-n150m-bail/

item.title: Peter Obi moved by El-Rufai’s wife’s plea to Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Peter Obi expresses concern over selective justice after watching Hajiya Asiya El-Rufai’s emotional appeal to Tinubu regarding her husband’s treatment.
Read More: https://punchng.com/peter-obi-moved-by-el-rufais-wifes-plea-to-tinubu/

item.title: Leeds sign free agent Wilson after Fulham exit
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Leeds United have completed a deal to sign Harry Wilson on a free transfer following his departure from Fulham.

item.title: VIDEO: Woman gives birth while shopping for baby items in Ibadan market
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A woman unexpectedly gave birth to a baby boy in Ibadan’s Gbagi market while shopping for baby items. Market women assisted in the safe delivery.
Read More: https://punchng.com/video-woman-gives-birth-while-shopping-for-baby-items-in-ibadan-market/

item.title: ADC Directs State Chapters To Forward Court Papers To National Headquarters
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) has directed all its state chapters and officials nationwide to immediately forward any court processes served on the party to its National Legal Adviser. The post ADC Directs State Chapters To Forward Court Papers To National Headquarters appeared first on Tren

item.title: Court of Appeal adjourns ADC deregistration case to July 14
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Court of Appeal adjourns hearing of ADC and four parties’ deregistration appeals after parties failed to exchange required court processes. The post Court of Appeal adjourns ADC deregistration case to July 14 appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: From AbokiFX to HyperFX: Instant FX settlement built on stablecoin rails
item.dc:creator: Premium Times

item.title: FIFA probes alleged racist abuse targeting IShowSpeed
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
FIFA has launched an investigation into alleged racist insults directed at popular streamer IShowSpeed during World Cup matches. FIFA condemns racism.

item.title: Gas Demand To Overtake Supply By 2030

item.description: …As FG Pushes Market Reforms, Affordable LPG The Federal Government has warned that Nigeria’s gas demand was expected to exceed supply by 2030, underscoring the need for urgent investments, market reforms and infrastructure expansion to bridge the widening gap, while ensuring cooking gas

item.title: Russia bans diesel exports as Ukraine attacks fuel refineries
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Russia has banned diesel exports until July 31 in a bid to ease fuel shortages linked to intensified Ukrainian drone attacks on the country’s energy infrastructure. Deputy Prime Minister Alexander Novak announced the measure on Wednesday during a government meeting chaired by President Vladimi

item.title: From Barcelona to Bromley: Inside Putellas' move to LCL

item.description: London City Lionesses have pulled off one of the biggest signings in WSL history - but how did they persuade Alexia Putellas to join?

item.title: VIDEO: Pregnant woman gives birth while shopping for baby items in Ibadan market
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: A pregnant woman gave birth to a baby boy at Gbagi market in Ibadan, Oyo State, after she went into labour while shopping for items for her unborn child. The incident happened on Monday evening at the busy market, where the woman had gone to buy baby essentials before she suddenly began experiencing

item.title: Cockroaches will learn to fear my SwitchBot Bot Rechargeable
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: A little robotic switch-flipper has become my sidekick in combating cockroaches. Before I got the SwitchBot Bot Rechargeable, I'd tiptoe through the dark every morning, hoping I wouldn't step on one of those terrible bugs scurrying around as I made my way to the light switch across the room. Now I'm

item.title: 'Unbelievable!' - Britain's Fery stuns Cobolli to reach Wimbledon semi-finals
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Britain's Arthur Fery stunned Flavio Cobolli in straight sets to continue his remarkable wild-card run and reach the semi-finals of Wimbledon.

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Marta Kostyuk delivered a career-best performance on grass to defeat Jasmine Paolini 6-3, 6-2 and book her place in the Wimbledon semifinals for the first time. The 24-year-old Ukrainian, who had never won a WTA Tour-level quarterfinal on grass before Wednesday, overpowered the 2024 finalist in just

item.title: Meta wants its AI glasses to seem less creepy. Its AI strategy says otherwise.
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Meta is adding a new safeguard to stop people from secretly recording others with its AI glasses. But the update comes as the company continues to expand how much personal data its AI products collect and use.

Amorim has stayed silent since leaving Old Trafford and opted against making any statements about his 14 months at Old Trafford

item.title: If Microsoft sold off Xbox, who would even buy it?
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: This week, Microsoft took a huge ax to its Xbox business. The company announced that it would be laying off 1,600 workers now, 1,600 more over the next fiscal year, and that it would be shedding four studios. Xbox CEO Asha Sharma hasn't been shy about why she's making such dramatic cuts, saying in a

item.title: London City Lionesses Sign 2-time Ballon d’Or Winner Putellas
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: London City Lionesses have secured the signing of Spanish midfielder Alexia Putellas, a two-time Ballon d’Or winner, on a three-year contract, marking one of the most significant transfers in women’s football history. Putellas, 32, joined the Women’s Super League club on a free tra

item.title: OpenAI releases new voice models for more natural live conversations
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: OpenAI says its new voice mode can speak and listen at the same time, a key ability for live translation.

item.title: ChatGPT’s upgraded voice mode is better at shutting up
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: OpenAI is overhauling ChatGPT's voice mode with a new model that it says is more like "talking to another person." The new GPT-Live-1 is designed to interrupt you less and will also wait for you to continue speaking if you pause mid-conversation. During a press briefing, OpenAI research lead Kundan

item.title: R&A issues new fan conduct guidelines to avoid bad behaviour at The Open
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: The R&A has launched a new fan code of conduct ahead of The Open at Royal Birkdale, with spectators at risk of being removed from the course for serious or repeated breaches.

item.title: Tinubu’s Policies Improved Benue Finances, Infrastructure – Alia
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: …okays state police, vows prudent use of allocations for security, agriculture, others …declares local govt autonomy working Benue State Governor, Rev. Fr. Hyacinth Alia, has declared that President Bola Tinubu’s economic policies have improved his state’s finances, security,

item.title: 30 vessels to arrive Lagos ports with petroleum products, other items
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Nigerian Ports Authority (NPA) says 33 ships carrying petroleum products, food items, and other goods are expected at Apapa, Lekki Deep Sea Port, and Tin Can Island Port in Lagos. The post 30 vessels to arrive Lagos ports with petroleum products, other items appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Police arraign varsity don over remarks on Katsina insecurity
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: An Ahmadu Bello University lecturer, Dr Bashir Kurfi, is arraigned over remarks on Katsina insecurity and remanded in police custody. The post Police arraign varsity don over remarks on Katsina insecurity appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Army Graduates 3,000 Recruits To Boost Fight Against Insecurity
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Nigerian Army has graduated more than 3,000 newly trained recruits as part of its ongoing expansion of manpower to strengthen national security and tackle the country's evolving security challenges. The post Army Graduates 3,000 Recruits To Boost Fight Against Insecurity appeared first on Trendi

item.title: BofA extends first $520 million loan to OpenAI ahead of IPO, source says
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.title: France vs Morocco: We must be more efficient in attack — Deschamps
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: According to Reuters, Deschamps said France's potent attack must make better use of its chances despite scoring a tournament-leading 14 goals ahead of the last-eight clash. The post France vs Morocco: We must be more efficient in attack — Deschamps appeared first on Vanguard News.
